info.name: Erawan Cluster API (Go)
info.description:
Postman collection for HAProxy, MySQL cluster, and PostgreSQL cluster endpoints.

**Required variables — fill these in Collection > Variables before sending any request:**
- `API_KEY` — sent as `X-API-Key` header on every request (collection auth)
- `ENCRYPTION_KEY` — 64-char hex string (AES-256-GCM). When set the pre-request script encrypts every request body and the test script decrypts every JSON response automatically. Generate with: `openssl rand -hex 32`

Leave `ENCRYPTION_KEY` empty to send/receive plain JSON (development only).

**Decrypted responses** are logged to the Postman console and stored in the `LAST_RESPONSE` collection variable for inspection.

variable.description: [REQUIRED] API key — sent as X-API-Key header on every request
[REQUIRED] 64-char hex string (32 bytes, AES-256-GCM). Generate: openssl rand -hex 32. Leave empty for plain JSON (dev only).
Job ID — auto-filled from Deploy response, or copy manually from List Jobs
Last decrypted JSON response — inspect after any request when ENCRYPTION_KEY is set
Grant the new user full superuser privileges (true) or scope to new_db only (false)
HAProxy listen port for PostgreSQL (set when creating the pgsql HAProxy config)
PostgreSQL superuser — auto-filled from Deploy response
PostgreSQL superuser password — auto-filled from Deploy response secret
Username to create or delete via DB Manager
Password for the new DB Manager user
Database name to create or delete via DB Manager
IP of the node to add or remove from the cluster
Job ID returned by Add Member / Remove Member — use this to poll the member operation. JOB_ID always stays as the deploy job ID.
Job ID returned by Recover Job — use this to poll the recovery operation. JOB_ID always stays as the original deploy job ID.

exec: // Runs automatically for every request that has a raw JSON body.
// Set ENCRYPTION_KEY (64 hex chars) to enable; leave empty for plain JSON.
console.warn('[erawan] ENCRYPTION_KEY is not set — sending plain JSON (dev mode)');
console.error('[erawan] ENCRYPTION_KEY must be 64 hex chars (32 bytes) — request NOT encrypted');
const cryptoKey = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', keyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['encrypt']);
const encrypted = await crypto.subtle.encrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: nonce }, cryptoKey, plaintext);
console.log('[erawan] request encrypted (' + plaintext.length + ' → ' + payload.length + ' bytes)');
// When ENCRYPTION_KEY is set the server returns {"data":"<encrypted>"};
// this script decrypts it, logs to console, and stores in LAST_RESPONSE.
// Binary response (e.g. zip download) — nothing to decrypt
console.error('[erawan] ENCRYPTION_KEY must be 64 hex chars — response NOT decrypted');
for (let i = 0; i < binary.length; i++) payload[i] = binary.charCodeAt(i);
const cryptoKey = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', keyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const dec = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: nonce }, cryptoKey, ciphertextWithTag);
// Recovery job — store separately, keep JOB_ID as the deploy job ID
// Member operation job — store separately, keep JOB_ID as the deploy job ID
if (s.admin_user) { pm.collectionVariables.set('MYSQL_DB_USER', s.admin_user); console.log('[erawan] MYSQL_DB_USER set to:', s.admin_user); }
if (s.postgres_user) { pm.collectionVariables.set('PGSQL_DB_USER', s.postgres_user); console.log('[erawan] PGSQL_DB_USER set to:', s.postgres_user); }

request.description:
Creates (or replaces) an HAProxy frontend+backend for a MySQL cluster.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `port` | int | yes | HAProxy frontend listen port (e.g. `25041`) |
| `node_ips` | string[] or string | yes | DB node IPs to add as backends |
| `db_port` | int | yes | MySQL port on the DB nodes (e.g. `3306` or Router `6446`) |

Adds a new backend server to an existing MySQL HAProxy config.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `port` | int | yes | HAProxy frontend port that identifies the config |
| `node_ip` | string | yes | IP of the new backend node to add |

Creates (or replaces) an HAProxy frontend for a PostgreSQL/Patroni cluster. Uses `httpchk GET /leader` on the Patroni REST port — only the current leader receives connections.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `port` | int | yes | HAProxy frontend listen port (e.g. `25042`) |
| `node_ips` | string[] or string | yes | DB node IPs to add as backends |
| `db_port` | int | yes | PostgreSQL port on the DB nodes (default `5432`) |
| `patroni_port` | int | no | Patroni REST port for `/leader` health check (default `8008`) |

Adds a new backend server to an existing PostgreSQL HAProxy config. The new node's Patroni health check is also configured.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `port` | int | yes | HAProxy frontend port that identifies the config |
| `node_ip` | string | yes | IP of the new backend node to add |

Removes an HAProxy tenant config file and reloads HAProxy. All traffic on that port is dropped.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `port` | int | yes | HAProxy frontend port identifying the config to delete |

Downloads all tenant HAProxy `.cfg` files packaged as a single zip archive.

The response is binary (`application/zip`). Use **Send and Download** in Postman to save the file.

exec: // Auto-extract JOB_ID and credentials from deploy response (no encryption)

request.description:
Starts a MySQL InnoDB Cluster deployment. Returns a job immediately (`202 Accepted`). Poll **Get Job** to track progress. `JOB_ID` is auto-extracted from the response into the collection variable.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `cluster_name` | string | yes | InnoDB Cluster name (alphanumeric, no spaces) |
| `primary_ip` | string | yes | IP of the primary node |
| `standby_ips` | string[] | yes | IPs of secondary nodes; `[]` for single-node |
| `admin_username` | string | yes | Cluster admin user to create (e.g. `clusteradmin`) |
| `admin_password` | string | yes | Password for the admin user (auto-generated if empty) |
| `new_user` | string | no | Application user to create |
| `new_user_password` | string | no | Password for the application user |
| `new_user_ssl_required` | bool | no | Require SSL for the application user. Omit to use default (`true`) |
| `new_user_superuser` | bool | no | Grant full server-level superuser privileges (`true`) or scope to `new_db` only (`false`). Default `true` |
| `new_db` | string | no | Application database to create |
| `assume_prepared` | bool | no | Skip node preparation steps if already prepared (default `false`) |
| `bootstrap_router` | bool | no | Bootstrap MySQL Router on each node (default `true`) |
| `ssh_port` | int | no | SSH port for Ansible (default `22`) |
| `mysql_port` | int | no | MySQL port on DB nodes (default `3306`) |
| `mysql_version` | int | no | Major version: `8` = 8.x, `9` = 9.x (default `8`) |
| `step_timeout_seconds` | int | no | Per-step Ansible timeout in seconds (default `900`) |

Collects live metrics from a running MySQL InnoDB Cluster. All SQL connections route through HAProxy — never directly to DB node IPs.

**Connection routing (server-side only — not in payload):**
- `host` always resolves to `PROXY_HOST` env on the server (default `127.0.0.1`)
- `user` / `password` are resolved from the deploy job via `job_id`
- `proxy_port` is the **HAProxy frontend port**, not the MySQL server port (`3306`)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID — resolves credentials and proxy routing |
| `proxy_port` | int | yes | HAProxy frontend port for this cluster (e.g. `25041`) |
| `database` | string | no | Default database context (default `information_schema`) |
| `ssl_mode` | string | no | `disable` or `require` (default `disable`) |
| `connect_timeout` | int | no | Connection timeout in seconds (default `10`) |
| `limit` | int | no | Top-N rows for slow queries and digest lists (default `20`) |
| `from` | string | no | ISO 8601 start time to filter slow queries |
| `to` | string | no | ISO 8601 end time to filter slow queries |
| `databases` | string[] | no | DB names to scope per-database metrics; `[]` = all user DBs |
| `categories` | string[] | no | Metric categories to collect; `[]` = all 7 |

**`categories` values:** `cluster`, `uptime`, `connections`, `replication`, `performance`, `query`, `maintenance`

exec: // Auto-extract credentials from plain-JSON get-job response (no encryption)
if (s.admin_user) { pm.collectionVariables.set('MYSQL_DB_USER', s.admin_user); console.log('[erawan] MYSQL_DB_USER set to:', s.admin_user); }

request.description:
Resumes a failed MySQL cluster job from the last completed step. Requires the `X-Job-Secret` header (returned in the deploy response).

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `admin_password` | string | no | Cluster admin password — leave empty to reuse the stored password from the original deploy |
| `new_user_password` | string | no | Application user password — leave empty to reuse from deploy |

exec: // Auto-extract RECOVERY_JOB_ID from recover response (no encryption).
// JOB_ID is intentionally NOT updated — it stays as the original deploy job ID.
console.log('[erawan] Poll GET /cluster/mysql/jobs/' + body.data.id + ' to track progress');

request.description:
Triggers a post-outage recovery for a MySQL InnoDB Cluster. Runs `boot_recovery` (`dba.rebootClusterFromCompleteOutage()`) and `bootstrap_router` (when configured) using the stored credentials — **no request body required**.

Use after a complete datacenter outage where all nodes went offline simultaneously. The cluster cannot reform automatically in this scenario; this endpoint is required to restore service.

Works on both `completed` and `failed` deploy jobs. Returns a new recovery job immediately (`202 Accepted`). Poll **Get Job** using `RECOVERY_JOB_ID` to track progress.

**Safe** — does not touch data files, user accounts, or databases. The worst outcome is the Ansible step fails and the job is marked `failed`; the endpoint can be called again.

Runs the rollback playbook to undo a failed MySQL cluster deployment. Dissolves the InnoDB Cluster, removes the admin user, stops and unregisters MySQL Router. Requires the `X-Job-Secret` header.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `admin_password` | string | no | Cluster admin password — leave empty to reuse from deploy |

exec: // Auto-extract MEMBER_JOB_ID from add-member response (no encryption).
// JOB_ID is intentionally NOT updated — it stays as the deploy job ID
// for DB manager / metrics / resume calls.
console.log('[erawan] Poll GET /cluster/mysql/jobs/' + body.data.id + ' to track progress');

request.description:
Adds one or more standby nodes to an existing MySQL InnoDB Cluster. Returns a new member job immediately (`202 Accepted`). Poll **Get Job** to track progress.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID that owns this cluster (resolves credentials) |
| `member_ips` | string[] | yes | IPs of the nodes to add as secondaries |
| `assume_prepared` | bool | no | Skip instance preparation steps if already prepared (default `false`) |

exec: // Auto-extract MEMBER_JOB_ID from remove-member response (no encryption).
// JOB_ID is intentionally NOT updated — it stays as the deploy job ID
// for DB manager / metrics / resume calls.
console.log('[erawan] Poll GET /cluster/mysql/jobs/' + body.data.id + ' to track progress');

request.description:
Removes a standby node from the MySQL InnoDB Cluster. Returns a new member job immediately (`202 Accepted`). Poll **Get Job** to track progress.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID that owns this cluster (resolves credentials) |
| `member_ip` | string | yes | IP of the standby node to remove |
| `force` | bool | no | Force removal even if node is unreachable (default `false`) |

item.name: Cluster - PostgreSQL
item.description: PostgreSQL supports either a single primary node or a primary with one or more standby nodes.

exec: // Auto-extract JOB_ID and credentials from plain-JSON deploy response (no encryption)
console.log('[erawan] PGSQL_DB_PASSWORD auto-filled from deploy secret');

request.description:
Starts a PostgreSQL/Patroni cluster deployment. Returns a job immediately (`202 Accepted`). Poll **Get Job** to track progress. `JOB_ID` is auto-extracted from the response into the collection variable.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `cluster_name` | string | yes | Patroni cluster scope name (alphanumeric, no spaces) |
| `primary_ip` | string | yes | IP of the primary node |
| `standby_ips` | string[] | yes | IPs of standby nodes; `[]` for single-node |
| `admin_username` | string | yes | Patroni admin user to create (e.g. `admin`) |
| `admin_password` | string | yes | Password for the admin user (auto-generated if empty) |
| `postgres_version` | int | no | PostgreSQL major version (default `16`) |
| `postgres_port` | int | no | PostgreSQL port on DB nodes (default `5432`) |
| `new_user` | string | no | Application user to create |
| `new_user_password` | string | no | Password for the application user |
| `new_user_ssl_required` | bool | no | Require SSL for the application user (default `true`) |
| `new_user_superuser` | bool | no | Grant full superuser privileges — `SUPERUSER CREATEDB CREATEROLE REPLICATION BYPASSRLS` (`true`) or `NOINHERIT NOSUPERUSER NOCREATEDB NOCREATEROLE NOREPLICATION NOBYPASSRLS` (`false`). Default `true` |
| `new_db` | string | no | Application database to create |
| `ssh_port` | int | no | SSH port for Ansible (default `22`) |
| `step_timeout_seconds` | int | no | Per-step Ansible timeout in seconds (default `900`) |

Collects live metrics from a running PostgreSQL/Patroni cluster. All SQL connections route through HAProxy — never directly to DB node IPs.

**Connection routing (server-side only — not in payload):**
- `host` always resolves to `PROXY_HOST` env on the server (default `127.0.0.1`)
- `user` / `password` are resolved from the deploy job via `job_id`
- `proxy_port` is the **HAProxy frontend port**, not the PostgreSQL server port (`5432`)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID — resolves credentials and proxy routing |
| `proxy_port` | int | yes | HAProxy frontend port for this cluster (e.g. `25042`) |
| `database` | string | no | Database to connect to (default `postgres`) |
| `ssl_mode` | string | no | `disable` or `require` (default `disable`) |
| `connect_timeout` | int | no | Connection timeout in seconds (default `10`) |
| `limit` | int | no | Top-N rows for slow queries and digest lists (default `20`) |
| `from` | string | no | ISO 8601 start time to filter slow queries |
| `to` | string | no | ISO 8601 end time to filter slow queries |
| `node_ips` | string[] | no | Patroni node IPs for cluster/failover category REST calls |
| `categories` | string[] | no | Metric categories to collect; `[]` = all 8 |

**`categories` values:** `cluster`, `uptime`, `connections`, `replication`, `performance`, `query`, `maintenance`, `failover`

Resumes a failed PostgreSQL cluster job from the last completed step. Requires the `X-Job-Secret` header (returned in the deploy response).

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `admin_password` | string | no | Patroni admin password — leave empty to reuse the stored password from the original deploy |
| `new_user_password` | string | no | Application user password — leave empty to reuse from deploy |

exec: // Auto-extract RECOVERY_JOB_ID from recover response (no encryption).
// JOB_ID is intentionally NOT updated — it stays as the original deploy job ID.
console.log('[erawan] Poll GET /cluster/pgsql/jobs/' + body.data.id + ' to track progress');

request.description:
Triggers a post-outage recovery for a PostgreSQL/Patroni cluster. Runs `cluster_bootstrap` (re-registers the cluster in DCS and restarts Patroni) and `verify_cluster` using stored credentials — **no request body required**.

Use after a complete datacenter outage where Patroni stopped on all nodes. Patroni cannot automatically elect a primary after a full outage; this endpoint re-bootstraps it safely without touching data directories (`standby_config` / `pg_basebackup` is never run).

Works on both `completed` and `failed` deploy jobs. Returns a new recovery job immediately (`202 Accepted`). Poll **Get Job** using `RECOVERY_JOB_ID` to track progress. Once the recovery job completes, HAProxy health checks will pass and metrics will resume automatically.

**Safe** — does not run `initdb`, `pg_basebackup`, or any DDL. The worst outcome is the Ansible step fails and the job is marked `failed`; the endpoint can be called again.

exec: // Auto-extract MEMBER_JOB_ID from add-member response (no encryption).
// JOB_ID is intentionally NOT updated — it stays as the deploy job ID
// for DB manager / metrics / resume calls.
console.log('[erawan] Poll GET /cluster/mysql/jobs/' + body.data.id + ' to track progress');

request.description:
Adds one or more standby nodes to an existing PostgreSQL/Patroni cluster. New nodes join the etcd cluster and clone data via `pg_basebackup`. Returns a new member job immediately (`202 Accepted`).

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID that owns this cluster (resolves credentials) |
| `member_ips` | string[] | yes | IPs of the nodes to add as standbys |

exec: // Auto-extract MEMBER_JOB_ID from remove-member response (no encryption).
// JOB_ID is intentionally NOT updated — it stays as the deploy job ID
// for DB manager / metrics / resume calls.
console.log('[erawan] Poll GET /cluster/mysql/jobs/' + body.data.id + ' to track progress');

request.description:
Removes a standby node from the PostgreSQL/Patroni cluster. Gracefully stops Patroni and etcd on the node, then removes it from the etcd cluster. Returns a new member job immediately (`202 Accepted`).

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID that owns this cluster (resolves credentials) |
| `member_ip` | string | yes | IP of the standby node to remove |
| `force` | bool | no | Force removal even if node is unreachable (default `false`) |

item.name: DB Manager - MySQL
item.description:
Create and delete users and databases on a running MySQL cluster.

Admin credentials: use MYSQL_DB_USER / MYSQL_DB_PASSWORD (clusteradmin) from the deploy response, or any user with the required privileges.

Privilege model:
- New users get SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, DROP, ALTER, INDEX, REFERENCES on the specified database only
- If no `database` is provided, the user is created with no database access
- Users cannot drop other users (no CREATE USER privilege)
- New databases are granted to all existing app users automatically

request.description:
Creates (or updates) a MySQL user. Credentials and primary IP are resolved from the deploy job. Idempotent — re-running with the same username updates the password and grants.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID (resolves admin credentials and primary IP) |
| `username` | string | yes | MySQL username to create |
| `password` | string | yes | User password |
| `superuser` | bool | no | `true` = grant `ALL PRIVILEGES ON *.*` + all dynamic privileges (same as deploy-time superuser). `false` = scoped grants on `database` only (default `false`) |
| `ssl_required` | bool | no | Require SSL for this user. Omit to use default (`true`) |
| `database` | string | no | Database to grant scoped access on (ignored when `superuser: true`) |

Renames a MySQL user. Existing grants are carried over automatically.

**Protected** — cannot be renamed: built-in system users (`root`, `mysql.sys`, `mysql.session`, `mysql.infoschema`) and the cluster admin user created at deploy time. API-created superusers can be renamed freely.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `username` | string | yes | Current username |
| `new_username` | string | yes | New username |

Resets the password for an existing MySQL user. Built-in system users (`root`, `mysql.sys`, `mysql.session`, `mysql.infoschema`) are refused.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `username` | string | yes | Username whose password to reset |
| `password` | string | yes | New password |

Drops a MySQL user.

**Protected** — cannot be dropped: built-in system users (`root`, `mysql.sys`, `mysql.session`, `mysql.infoschema`) and the cluster admin user created at deploy time. API-created superusers can be dropped freely.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `username` | string | yes | Username to drop |

Creates a MySQL database and grants all existing non-system app users `ALL PRIVILEGES` on it. Credentials resolved from the deploy job.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `dbname` | string | yes | Database name to create |

Renames a MySQL database by creating the new database, moving all tables, re-applying grants, and dropping the old database.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `dbname` | string | yes | Current database name |
| `new_dbname` | string | yes | New database name |

Terminates all active connections to the database, then drops it. System databases (`information_schema`, `mysql`, `performance_schema`, `sys`) are refused.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `dbname` | string | yes | Database name to drop |

item.name: DB Manager - PostgreSQL
item.description:
Create and delete users and databases on a running PostgreSQL/Patroni cluster.

Admin credentials: use PGSQL_DB_USER / PGSQL_DB_PASSWORD (postgres superuser) from the deploy response.

Privilege model:
- New roles get LOGIN, CREATEDB, NOSUPERUSER, NOCREATEROLE
- Granted USAGE + CREATE on schema public; SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, TRUNCATE on all tables of the specified database only; ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES for cross-user future tables
- If no `database` is provided, the role is created with no database access
- Cannot delete other roles (NOCREATEROLE)
- New databases are granted to all existing app users automatically

request.description:
Creates (or updates) a PostgreSQL role. Credentials and primary IP are resolved from the deploy job. Idempotent — re-running with the same username updates the password and grants.

SSL enforcement is applied via Patroni's `pg_hba` DCS config (not at the role level). The pg_hba rule for this user is always written and kept in sync with `ssl_required`.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID (resolves admin credentials and primary IP) |
| `username` | string | yes | PostgreSQL role name to create |
| `password` | string | yes | Role password |
| `superuser` | bool | no | `true` = `LOGIN SUPERUSER CREATEDB CREATEROLE REPLICATION BYPASSRLS` (same as deploy-time superuser). `false` = `NOSUPERUSER NOCREATEROLE INHERIT` scoped to `database` (default `false`) |
| `ssl_required` | bool | no | `true` = `hostssl` + `hostnossl reject` in `pg_hba.conf`. `false` = plain `host` rule. Omit to use default (`true`). Patroni DCS is patched automatically |
| `database` | string | no | Database to grant scoped access on (ignored when `superuser: true`) |

Renames a PostgreSQL role. Existing grants are carried over automatically.

**Protected** — cannot be renamed: `postgres`, any `pg_*` built-in role, replication roles (e.g. `replicator`), and the cluster admin user created at deploy time. API-created superusers can be renamed freely.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `username` | string | yes | Current role name |
| `new_username` | string | yes | New role name |

Resets the password for an existing PostgreSQL role. Built-in system roles (`postgres`, `pg_*` prefix) are refused.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `username` | string | yes | Role name whose password to reset |
| `password` | string | yes | New password |

Drops a PostgreSQL role. Objects owned by the role are reassigned to the admin user before drop.

**Protected** — cannot be dropped: `postgres`, any `pg_*` built-in role, replication roles (e.g. `replicator`), and the cluster admin user created at deploy time. API-created superusers can be dropped freely.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `username` | string | yes | Role name to drop |

Creates a PostgreSQL database and grants all existing non-system app roles `CONNECT` and `ALL ON ALL TABLES` access. Credentials resolved from the deploy job.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `dbname` | string | yes | Database name to create |

Renames a PostgreSQL database. Active connections are terminated first. Existing grants are preserved automatically by PostgreSQL's internal OID references.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `dbname` | string | yes | Current database name |
| `new_dbname` | string | yes | New database name |

Terminates all active connections via `pg_terminate_backend()`, then drops the database. System databases (`postgres`, `template0`, `template1`) are refused.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes | Deploy job ID |
| `dbname` | string | yes | Database name to drop |
